name: SAMPL
description: Statistical Analyses and Methods in the Published Literature — basic statistical reporting.

items.title: Pre-analysis plan
items.description: Describe the study design and indicate whether a pre-specified analysis plan was developed.

items.title: Sample size justification
items.description: Provide a justification for the sample size, including assumptions used (effect size, power, alpha).

items.title: Description of statistical methods
items.description: Describe the statistical methods used to analyse the primary and secondary outcomes.

items.title: Software used
items.description: Identify the statistical software (name, version, manufacturer) used for the analyses.

items.title: Variables and measurement
items.description: Describe how all variables were defined and measured; for continuous variables, indicate the unit and precision.

items.title: Assumptions of statistical tests
items.description: Verify and describe how the assumptions of statistical tests (e.g. normality, equal variances) were assessed.

items.title: Handling of missing data
items.description: Describe how missing data were handled (e.g. complete case, imputation), with rationale.

items.title: Multiple comparisons
items.description: Describe how multiple comparisons were addressed, if applicable.

items.title: Subgroup and sensitivity analyses
items.description: Describe any subgroup and sensitivity analyses and whether they were pre-specified or post-hoc.

items.title: Outliers and influential observations
items.description: Describe how outliers and influential observations were identified and handled.

items.title: Descriptive statistics: means
items.description: Report means with appropriate standard deviations (not standard errors) for normally distributed data.

items.title: Descriptive statistics: medians
items.description: Report medians with interquartile ranges or full ranges for non-normally distributed data.

items.title: Descriptive statistics: counts and percentages
items.description: Report counts and percentages for categorical data, with denominators clearly identified.

items.title: Confidence intervals
items.description: Report confidence intervals (typically 95%) for effect estimates rather than just p-values.

items.title: Exact p-values
items.description: Report exact p-values (e.g. p = 0.034) rather than thresholds (e.g. p < 0.05).

items.title: Effect sizes
items.description: Report effect sizes with appropriate measures of precision for primary and secondary outcomes.

items.title: Regression analyses
items.description: For regression analyses, report the model, included variables, estimates, standard errors, and goodness-of-fit measures.

items.title: Survival analyses
items.description: For survival analyses, report event counts, follow-up time, hazard ratios with confidence intervals, and verify proportional hazards assumption.

items.title: Correlation analyses
items.description: For correlation, report the correlation coefficient, sample size, and confidence interval; clarify the correlation type (Pearson, Spearman).

items.title: Diagnostic accuracy
items.description: For diagnostic studies, report sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, likelihood ratios with confidence intervals.

items.title: Agreement statistics
items.description: For agreement studies, report Cohen's kappa or intraclass correlation coefficient with confidence intervals.

items.title: Tables
items.description: Tables present data clearly with appropriate units, sample sizes, and uncertainty measures.

items.title: Figures
items.description: Figures display data appropriately with clear axis labels, units, and error bars where applicable; specify what error bars represent.

items.title: Numbers and percentages reported
items.description: Numbers are reported with appropriate precision; percentages are accompanied by raw counts.

items.title: Statistical significance vs. clinical relevance
items.description: Distinguish statistical significance from clinical relevance; do not interpret a non-significant result as evidence of no effect.

items.title: Limitations of statistical methods
items.description: Discuss limitations of the statistical methods used, including any deviations from the pre-analysis plan.

items.title: Generalisability
items.description: Discuss the generalisability of the findings to populations beyond the study sample.

items.title: Reproducibility
items.description: Provide sufficient detail (data, code, parameters) to allow reproduction of the analyses.

items.title: Data availability
items.description: State whether the underlying data are available, and where.

items.title: Code availability
items.description: State whether the analysis code is available, and where.
